This comes as Amazon lags behind its satellite launch schedule. It faces strong competition from Elon Musk's Starlink. Amazon aims to offer significantly more capacity than Starlink in India.

items.title: SC grills Sebi over lingering probe against Sandesara brothers despite Rs 5,100 cr settlement
items.summary: In a surprising turn of events, the Supreme Court has scrutinised Sebi's ongoing actions against the directors of Sterling Biotech. This review arises following the directors’ hefty ₹5,100 crore settlement payment. Earlier, the court had invalidated all related cases. Sebi's investigation concerns the suspected routing of funds.

items.title: Exporters, freight forwarders seek government help as war surcharges on cargo surge
items.summary: Amid escalating geopolitical tensions, Indian exporters and freight forwarders are appealing for government support. Shipping lines worldwide are imposing significant war-risk surcharges on Middle Eastern shipments, often doubling the previously established rates.

items.title: Magicbricks and realtors' body tie up for data-driven info exchange
items.summary: Magicbricks and NAREDCO have joined forces to forge a strategic alliance designed to invigorate India's real estate sector. This one-year agreement positions Magicbricks as a key knowledge and communication partner, tasked with launching research reports and regular newsletters.

items.title: HC upholds SECI's reallocation of solar capacity to Adani Co
items.summary: The Delhi High Court has backed Solar Energy Corporation of India's 2023 move. This decision reallocated 2.33 gigawatts of solar power capacity from Azure Power India to Adani Green Energy. A public interest litigation challenging this reallocation was dismissed. The court found no evidence of loss to the public exchequer. The reallocation involved a reduction in the power purchase price.

items.title: Bank-NBFC co-lending pacts may be expanded to aid MSMEs
items.summary: A parliamentary panel suggests boosting bank and NBFC co-lending to help MSMEs access cheaper credit. It also proposes listing profitable regional rural banks and reinforcing state-run general insurers. The committee highlighted a significant credit gap for small businesses and a decline in insurance penetration. Recommendations include developing climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rural areas.

items.title: At bottom of the pyramid, 21 million small borrowers have dropped out
items.summary: Twenty-one million small borrowers exited formal finance. Lenders stopped new loans to those with overdue payments after a microfinance crisis. Many have returned to private moneylenders. This trend reflects tighter lending rules and market contraction. Loan disbursements saw a recent increase, indicating a slow recovery.

items.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
items.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

items.title: RBI seeks banks’ views on AI-based facial recognition tools at ATMs, branches to curb fraud
items.summary: As per the draft guidelines, a bona fide victim, being an individual person and having lodged a complaint involving gross loss of an amount up to Rs 50,000 on account of fraudulent electronic banking transaction (s), would be compensated 85% of the net loss amount (calculated after reducing recoveries made, whether before or after paying the compensation, from the gross loss amount), or Rs 25,000, whichever is less, once during his or her lifetime.

items.title: Audit watchdog flags gaps in Big 4 firms PwC, EY, KPMG and BDO audit practices
items.summary: India's financial watchdog, NFRA, has released inspection reports on major audit firms. These reports highlight issues with audit independence, internal governance, and scrutiny of related-party transactions. The regulator aims to improve audit quality and protect investors. While some improvements were noted, further strengthening of controls is required across these firms.

The chamber also wants the RBI to resolve issues with letters of credit. Inconsistent handling of document discrepancies by banks is a concern. Unfair practices by rating agencies are also highlighted. Clearer guidelines for banks are requested.

items.title: West Asia war: Specialty fertiliser imports 10-20% costlier, cos to step up production

Dealers are also stocking these products as a hedge against potential shortages.

items.title: Despite PNG meter connection, nearly 40% customers can't access piped gas

items.summary: Stocks like Hindustan Zinc, Hindalco, Jindal Steel, JSW Steel and Tata Steel gained over 1 percent from previous close.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. It already raised Rs 348.52 crore through anchor investors' portion on Tuesday, the day before issue opening.

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
